Mango trees observed in Sawantwadi taluka in Sindhudurg district of Maharashtra India. Previously mango trees when mango trees were observed on 30th of October2021, no trees were sprouting new leaves. Next observations were noted on 4th of November 2021. That time few tender leaves were spotted on 4 trees out of 12. Next and latest observations were noted on 7th of November 2021. And as per those observations 22 trees out of 25 mango trees were sprouting new leaves. Now in few more days about in the mid of November all mango trees are expected to sprout new leaves. First picture is of 30th October 2021 where no sprouting is seen and the second picture if of 7th November 2021 where new tender leaves are seen which are reddish to light green in colour
whole tree wont be visible in this photo because i was in a bus at that time. but i was able to observe new lush green leaves.
i can observe some of the leaves are red in colour
This picture is clicked on 4th November 2021.Location: Insuli village, Sawantwadi, Sindhudurg district ,Maharashtra. No new leaves are seen yet. But they are expected in next few days.

Tree 1. Few tender leaves are spotted. Tender leaves are marked in white circles as they are not clearly visible. Location: Insuli village, Sawantwadi, Sindhudurg, Maharashtra
New leaves are sprouting. Tender leaves are red as well light green in colour.
Tree 3. Sprouting of new leaves is seen. Tender leaves are red and light green in colour. Red leaves are slightly bigger in size as compared to that of green leaves.

Sprouting of new leaves are seen. Location: Majgaon village Sawantwadi, Sindhudurg Maharashtra. This could be an exceptional case as other mango trees in this area aren't been sprouting new leaves yet.

Tree 4. Sprouting of new leaves. Tender leaves are marked in white circles. As per the observations of local people here every year mango tree firstly sprouts new leaves and then flowering and fruiting process takes place. According to them monsoon has great impact on these phases of tree.
